summary: Ukrainian forces destroyed seven UAVs targeting Moscow and reported 175 drones shot down across Russia on June 27. High‑intensity FAB‑500 bomb strikes hit Zaporizhzhia and other front‑line cities, causing civilian casualties. Russian forces continued offensive operations in multiple sectors, including Kharkiv, Sumy, and Belgorod, while defending against numerous Shahed‑type UAV attacks.
key_points: Seven UAVs heading for Moscow were destroyed and Russian MoD recorded 175 drones shot down on June 27 (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31204).
FAB‑500 aerial bombs were dropped on Ukrainian drone control centers in DPR, Dnepropetrovsk and Sumy regions (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31186).
Russian tactical aviation launched guided aviation bombs on Zaporizhzhia city, causing six injuries including a 13‑year‑old girl (Source: @UkraineNow/82217).
Shahed, Gerbera and other strike UAVs attacked from multiple directions, with Ukrainian air defence reporting 113 UAVs downed on the night of June 26‑27 (Source: @kpszsu/66555).
Ukrainian FPV drone strikes hit an enterprise in Shebekino (Belgorod) injuring civilians and killed a man in Ploskoye (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31204).
Russian forces reported repelling Ukrainian counter‑attacks in Kazachya Lopan and Vovchansk, taking prisoners (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31204).
Ukrainian forces captured strongholds on the Velykomihailovka‑Alexandrovka line in Dnipropetrovsk region (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31204).
Multiple UAV groups were observed operating over the Black Sea heading toward Chornomorsk and Odesa (Source: @eRadarrua/98337).

sections.executive_summary: Ukrainian air defences downed 175 drones and destroyed seven UAVs bound for Moscow on June 27, indicating intensified aerial contest. Russian tactical aviation employed FAB‑500 bombs on Zaporizhzhia and other targets, causing civilian injuries. Ground fighting persisted across the Sumy, Kharkiv, Belgorod and Dnipropetrovsk fronts with both sides reporting gains and losses.

points: Ukrainian forces reported destroying seven UAVs targeting Moscow and claimed 175 drones shot down by Russian MoD on June 27, reflecting robust air‑defence activity (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31204).
FPV drones were used to strike Russian enterprises in Shebekino (Belgorod) and Ploskoye, injuring civilians and killing a man, showing Ukraine's deep‑strike capability (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31204).
Ukrainian troops captured strongholds on the Velykomihailovka‑Alexandrovka line in Dnipropetrovsk region, expanding their foothold in the east (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31204).
In the Sumy direction, Ukrainian assault troops engaged in small‑arms battles in Bachevsk, Pysarevka and Novaya Sech, indicating ongoing offensive pressure (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31204).
Ukrainian forces reported repelling Russian counter‑attacks in Kazachya Lopan and taking prisoners, demonstrating defensive resilience (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31204).
Ukrainian air‑defence units shot down 113 Shahed‑type and other UAVs during the night of June 26‑27, neutralising a large wave of aerial threats (Source: @kpszsu/66555).
Ukrainian drone control centers in DPR, Dnepropetrovsk and Sumy were hit by Russian FAB‑500 bombs, indicating Ukrainian vulnerability to high‑payload strikes (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31186).
Russian MoD recorded 175 drones shot down across the country on June 27, reflecting extensive aerial activity (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31204).
Russian tactical aviation conducted guided aviation bomb strikes on Zaporizhzhia, injuring six civilians including a teenage girl (Source: @UkraineNow/82217).
Russian forces reported destroying two UAV‑guided “Geranium” locomotives under a bridge in Zaporozhye and three in Kharkiv region, targeting logistics (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31204).
In Belgorod region, a Ukrainian FPV drone detonated at an enterprise in Shebekino, injuring a civilian, highlighting Russian vulnerability to drone attacks (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31204).
Russian forces continued offensive operations on the Slavyansk direction from Ray‑Aleksandrovka, indicating sustained pressure in the south (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31204).
Russian troops reported destroying Ukrainian reserves and striking Oskol River crossings near Krasny Liman, showing defensive actions on the northern front (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31204).
Russian air defence claimed downing 3 long‑range “Flamingo” cruise missiles and 511 UAVs of airplane type on a single day (Source: @tass_agency/382639).
Ukrainian forces captured strongholds on the Velykomihailovka‑Alexandrovka line in Dnipropetrovsk region, shifting the front eastward (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31204).
In the eastern Zaporozhye sector, Ukrainian “Far Eastern warriors” wedged west of Vozdvyzhevka, Verkhnya Tersa and Komsomolske, forcing Russian reserves to spread (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31204).
Russian forces reported 18 strikes on Zaporozhye settlements in 24 hours, killing one and injuring four, indicating heavy combat in the east (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31204).
Ukrainian attacks on the Zaporozhye NPP infrastructure continued, showing strategic targeting of energy assets (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31204).
Shahed‑type UAV attacks originated from directions including Kursk, Bryansk and Crimea, impacting eastern sectors (Source: @kpszsu/66555).
Ukrainian FPV drone strikes hit an ambulance in Kherson’s Kakhovka district, killing a paramedic, reflecting cross‑front UAV threats (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31204).
Russian forces reported repelling Ukrainian counter‑attacks in the Velykomihailovka direction, maintaining control (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31204).
Ukrainian assault troops fought small‑arms battles in Bachevsk, Shostka district (Sumy) and surrounding villages, indicating active northern engagements (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31204).
Russian Shahed UAV wave on June 26‑27 included launches from Kursk and Bryansk, targeting northern Ukrainian areas (Source: @kpszsu/66555).
Ukrainian air‑defence downed 113 UAVs across north, south and east, showing widespread aerial contest (Source: @kpszsu/66555).
Ukrainian FPV drones were observed over the Black Sea heading toward Odesa and Chornomorsk, posing a northern‑southern maritime threat (Source: @eRadarrua/98337).
Russian forces reported destroying Ukrainian “Geranium” locomotives in Kharkiv region, affecting northern logistics lines (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31204).
Ukrainian UAV activity was noted in the Poltava region (Opishnia, Karlivka) as potential guidance platforms for strikes (Source: @kpszsu/66548).
Ukrainian forces reported repelling Russian counter‑attacks in Kazachya Lopan (Kharkiv) and taking prisoners, affecting the northern front dynamics (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31204).
Ukrainian FPV drone attacks on Zaporizhzhia caused six injuries, including a 13‑year‑old girl, after guided bombs struck the city (Source: @UkraineNow/82217).
Russian tactical aviation launched guided aviation bombs on Zaporizhzhia, resulting in civilian casualties (Source: @UkraineNow/82217).
Ukrainian strikes hit gas stations in Zaporizhzhia and Kharkiv regions, igniting a gas tank and damaging infrastructure (Source: @UkraineNow/82222).
In the Belgorod region, a Ukrainian FPV drone detonated at an enterprise in Shebekino, injuring a civilian (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31204).
Ukrainian forces reported fighting in the Vovchansk sector (Losevka, Ukrainske, Zemlyanoy Yar) on the Kharkiv direction, indicating southern‑eastern pressure (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31204).
Russian forces continued offensive operations on the Slavyansk direction from Ray‑Aleksandrovka, affecting the southern front (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31204).
Ukrainian UAV groups were observed over the Black Sea heading toward Chornomorsk and Odesa, suggesting maritime‑linked southern operations (Source: @eRadarrua/98337).
Russian MoD recorded 175 drones shot down across Russia on June 27, indicating high aerial activity (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31204).
Ukrainian air‑defence reported downing 113 Shahed‑type and other UAVs during the night of June 26‑27 (Source: @kpszsu/66555).
Russian tactical aviation employed guided aviation bombs on Zaporizhzhia, causing civilian injuries (Source: @UkraineNow/82217).
Russian air defence claimed intercepting three long‑range “Flamingo” cruise missiles and 511 UAVs of airplane type in a single day (Source: @tass_agency/382639).
Ukrainian forces used FPV drones to strike Russian enterprises in Shebekino and Ploskoye, demonstrating offensive UAV use (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31204).
Ukrainian UAV groups operating from the Black Sea towards Odesa and Chornomorsk were reported, indicating maritime aerial threats (Source: @eRadarrua/98337).
Russian forces reported destroying Ukrainian “Geranium” UAV‑guided locomotives under bridges in Zaporozhye and Kharkiv, targeting logistics (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31204).

key_developments: Ukrainian air‑defence downed 175 drones and destroyed seven UAVs bound for Moscow on June 27, showing heightened aerial contest (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31204).
Russian tactical aviation dropped FAB‑500 bombs on Zaporizhzhia, injuring six civilians including a teenage girl (Source: @UkraineNow/82217).
Shahed‑type UAV wave on June 26‑27 involved 129 strike drones from multiple Russian directions; Ukrainian forces shot down 113 (Source: @kpszsu/66555).
Ukrainian forces captured the Velykomihailovka‑Alexandrovka line in Dnipropetrovsk, expanding eastern front control (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31204).
Ukrainian FPV drones struck an enterprise in Shebekino (Belgorod) injuring civilians, indicating deep‑strike capability (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31204).
Russian air defence reported intercepting three “Flamingo” cruise missiles and 511 UAVs of airplane type in one day (Source: @tass_agency/382639).
Ukrainian small‑arms battles continued in Sumy’s Shostka district and surrounding villages, maintaining pressure on the northern front (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31204).
sections.threat_assessment: Ukrainian forces are leveraging a mix of FPV drones, guided aviation bombs and high‑payload FAB‑500 strikes to target Russian logistics, industrial sites and civilian infrastructure, increasing pressure on rear areas. Russia continues to employ Shahed‑type UAV swarms and long‑range cruise missiles, while defending key transport corridors with anti‑air assets. The proliferation of UAVs across the Black Sea, Poltava and Sumy regions expands the contested airspace, raising the risk of collateral damage and complicating command‑and‑control for both sides.
sections.regional_response: Russian authorities report extensive air‑defence engagements and claim successful interceptions of cruise missiles, suggesting a bolstered missile shield in response to intensified Ukrainian aerial attacks. Ukrainian command appears to prioritize deep‑strike UAV operations against Russian industrial targets and logistical nodes, while sustaining ground offensives in Sumy, Kharkiv and Dnipropetrovsk. Local civilian authorities in Zaporizhzhia and Kharkiv are coordinating emergency response to bombings and gas‑station fires, indicating heightened civil‑military coordination.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of UAV launch points in Kursk, Bryansk and Crimea is critical, as these corridors feed the Shahed swarm targeting northern Ukraine. The emergence of Ukrainian FPV drone strikes on Russian enterprises in Belgorod suggests a shift toward precision, low‑observable attacks on rear‑area assets. Continued Russian use of FAB‑500 bombs against urban centers underscores the high‑intensity kinetic phase of the conflict, requiring updated casualty and infrastructure impact assessments.
